event: chunk
data: {"content": "<think>\nThe user wants a"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " concise explanation of how spaced repetition strengthens long-term memory, in three short paragraphs. This is a straightforward explanatory question \u2014 no tools needed, no project attached, no skills to load. I should just answer directly, be clear and accurate"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": ", and use the cognitive science framing (encoding, retrieval, spacing effect, desirable difficulty, consolidation).\n\n"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": "Let me draft three short paragraphs:\n\n1. The core mechanism: spacing effect + retrieval practice. Each review"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " session forces active recall, which strengthens the memory trace. The expanding gaps align with the forgetting curve.\n\n2. The"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " neuroscience angle: reconsolidation, hippocampal-cortical transfer, synaptic changes. Each retrieval makes the memory temporarily labile and"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " re-stores it more durably.\n\n3. Practical payoff: how this compounds into durable, flexible knowledge"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " \u2014 and the contrast with cramming.\n\nKeep it tight, no preamble, no filler. I'll aim for roughly 3-5"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " sentences per paragraph.\n"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": "</think>\nSpaced repetition strengthens memory by exploiting the **spacing effect** \u2014 the finding that information reviewed at gradually increasing"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " intervals is retained far better than the same material crammed into one session. Each review forces *active recall* rather than passive re-reading, which acts"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " as a form of \"desirable difficulty\": the mental effort of pulling the answer back out strengthens the underlying memory trace. As the brain successfully"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " retrieves the item across sessions, the interval before the next review is extended, keeping the difficulty just high enough to prevent the material from slipping"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " below the threshold of recall.\n\nThe mechanism has a clear neural basis. Every successful retrieval briefly returns"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " the memory to a **labile state**, after which it must be re-stored \u2014 a process called *reconsolidation* \u2014 and each cycle"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " tends to make the trace more durable and less dependent on the hippocampus. Over time, repeated spaced retrievals help shift the memory toward"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " more distributed cortical networks, the signature of long-term, stable knowledge. This is why a single exposure decays"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " within days, but several well-spaced retrievals can lock something in for months or years.\n\nIn practice,"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " the cumulative effect is dramatic: short, frequent sessions separated by expanding gaps outperform long, intensive study blocks"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " of equal total time. Spaced repetition also produces more **flexible** memories \u2014 facts that can"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": " be applied in new contexts, not just recited on cue \u2014 because each retrieval slightly varies the cueing conditions"}

event: chunk
data: {"content": ". The result is learning that compounds: every review costs less effort than the last, and the material becomes increasingly resistant to forgetting."}

event: done
